title: Teej Vrat Katha
intro: The sacred story of Teej, a joyous festival celebrated by women for marital bliss, fertility, and family happiness.
description:
Teej is a vibrant festival celebrated primarily by women, especially in North and Central India. Women fast and worship Lord Shiva and Goddess Parvati on this day. The vrat katha of Teej is rich with spiritual significance.

|| The Story ||
It is said that Goddess Parvati performed intense penance to win the heart of Lord Shiva. After 108 years of tapasya, Lord Shiva was pleased and married her. This festival celebrates their divine union.

Women who observe the Teej fast with devotion are believed to be blessed with happy marriages and healthy families.
meaning: Teej celebrates the divine love between Shiva and Parvati. It is a festival of joy, dance, and the celebration of marital harmony and family well-being.

faq.a1: In the month of Shravan (July-August), on the third day of the waxing moon.
faq.a2: While fasting is traditional, the spirit of devotion and celebration is most important.
faq.a3: It celebrates womanhood, marital happiness, and the eternal love between Shiva and Parvati.
